frameworks.name: model-based reasoning
frameworks.one_line: agents that build and exploit their own internal models autonomously

frameworks.name: autonomous learning agents
frameworks.one_line: scaling human-like intelligence by minimizing hand-engineered priors

frameworks.name: ingredients of human-like intelligence
frameworks.one_line: Lake et al. key-ingredients list serves as the framing they respond to

core_question: Should human-like machine intelligence be built on hand-engineered ingredients or on agents that autonomously learn and exploit their own internal models?
key_assumptions: model-based reasoning is essential for human-like intelligence
ready-made formal models are unavailable for many real-world domains
autonomy (self-built models) scales better than hand engineering
manipulations: argumentative pivot: shift emphasis from prescribed ingredients to learned autonomy
survey of example systems progressing toward autonomous human-like abilities
key_findings: existing autonomous agents already exhibit progress toward human-like capabilities across several domains
outstanding challenges remain for scaling autonomous learning to real-world complexity
interpretations: autonomy should be added as a key ingredient alongside Lake et al.'s list
autonomous learning gives the greatest chance of success when formal models cannot be supplied
limitations_noted: outstanding challenges remain unresolved for scaling to real-world complexity
